original_hypothesis.title: WebAuthn User Verification Bypass via Hardcoded requireUV=false
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: CoinbaseSmartWallet._validateSignature calls WebAuthn.verify with requireUV: false hardcoded. This means the User Verified (UV) flag in authenticatorData is never checked. An attacker with physical access to an unlocked device containing the passkey can authorize arbitrary wallet operations (including fund transfers, owner changes, and upgrades) with just a physical tap (User Presence), without requiring biometric verification or PIN entry. This reduces passkey security from two-factor to single-factor for physical access scenarios.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The UV flag is the WebAuthn mechanism that ensures the user explicitly authenticated (biometric/PIN). Bypassing it means stolen/borrowed devices can be used to drain wallets without the owner's biometric consent.

proof_of_signal.pattern: WebAuthn.verify({..., requireUV: false, ...}) hardcoded, UV flag never enforced

preconditions: Attacker has physical access to the device containing the passkey
Device is unlocked or in a state where User Presence (tap) is sufficient

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: requireUV: false at line 300 permanently disables biometric/PIN verification for all passkey operations
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: No admin configuration available to enable UV checking

operational_questions: Is requireUV=false a deliberate design choice for UX reasons?
What percentage of wallets use passkey-only authentication?
Can UV enforcement be added without breaking existing passkey signatures?
validation_requirements: Create passkey signature without UV flag set and verify it's accepted
Test with authenticators that distinguish UP-only vs UV+UP responses
Assess real-world authenticator behavior when UV is not requested

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Obtain physical access to victim's device with passkey
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Trigger passkey signing with just a tap (no biometric needed)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Sign a UserOp transferring all funds or adding attacker as owner

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Full wallet takeover via owner addition
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Depends on whether other owners exist

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Passkey signatures without UV flag in authenticatorData

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
